title: Braised Australian Butter Stir-fry
description: A buttery, dense indulgent Australian dish featuring hot sauce and poached egg-free-friendly elements.

ingredients.name: Thousand Island Dressing (substitute for hot sauce)

instructions: Season chicken with salt and pepper on both sides. Dredge in flour and shake off excess.
Heat butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat until melted and shimmering.
Add chicken pieces, skin-side down, and cook for about 3-4 minutes or until golden brown.
Flip and continue to cook for another 2-3 minutes until desired doneness is achieved. Transfer cooked chicken to a plate.
In the same skillet, add red wine vinegar and Thousand Island Dressing (substitute for hot sauce). Stir well.
Return chicken pieces to the skillet along with any accumulated juices. Pour in enough liquid to cover half of the chicken.
Cover the skillet and place it in the preheated oven. Cook until all the liquid is absorbed, about 10-15 minutes or until tender.
Garnish with chopped fresh herbs if using and serve hot.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
